title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
The Significance of Vaccination - National Vaccination Day
The 16th of March¹ is celebrated as National Vaccination Day in India. The day recognises the efforts made by frontline workers who ensure that the women and children of the country are vaccinated against major illnesses. Moreover, various vaccination drives are undertaken to relieve the disease burden of the country.
While National Vaccination Day also emphasises the importance of vaccination, urging households to get vaccinated to eradicate diseases like smallpox, polio, Hepatitis, flu, etc. So, on this National Vaccination Day, here are ten reasons why you should get vaccinated –
To prevent diseases:
One of the primary reasons for vaccination is to avoid possible illnesses that might arise if you are not vaccinated. Illnesses like Hepatitis, typhoid, flu, polio, etc., can be prevented if you are vaccinated against them. Even recent illnesses like the Coronavirus infection can be kept at bay if you are vaccinated.
These diseases might cause severe physiological losses and are avoided if they can be through vaccines.
For overall safety:
Being vaccinated reduces the chances of common illnesses and keeps you safe. For children, vaccinations are a must for their overall health and development.
For immunity building:
Vaccines contain bacteria and viruses in dormant forms so that they can fight against similar germs in the case of an illness. Thus, with vaccines, you can make your body immune to particular kinds of diseases and stay healthy.
To reduce the probability of death:
Some illnesses, like COVID, for instance, can develop into serious medical complications that might even cause death. As such, being vaccinated against such deadly diseases can reduce the probability of death.
To reduce the probability of sickness:
Vaccines also reduce the possibility of falling sick and help maintain your health.
To avoid the possibility of infections:
Some diseases can be contagious. So, if one non-vaccinated individual catches such a disease, he/she can infect his/her family members and make them fall sick. In extreme cases, the family member can even lose their life or develop severe physiological conditions.
A very common example, again, is the COVID pandemic. Being highly infectious, an unvaccinated individual can place his whole family at risk of infection.
Vaccines are safe:
Every type of vaccine available in the country has been developed after proper research and trials. Moreover, the efficacy of the vaccine has also been endorsed by recognised medical councils and institutions. As such, vaccines are completely safe for everyone.
Vaccines are affordable:
Even when it comes to the cost factor, vaccines are affordable. Children and adults can get vaccinated at affordable rates and even free of cost in some cases.
To reduce the financial burden of a medical contingency:
In the case of an illness or disease, an individual not only suffers physiological trauma but financial losses too. Possible hospitalisations, treatments and the associated medical bills can be very expensive. Paying for such expensive medical costs can drain you of your savings or put a considerable strain on your finances.
To safeguard your loved ones:
Lastly, by vaccinating your family members, you can ensure their health. You can keep them away from preventable diseases. Moreover, when you are also vaccinated, you can be there for your family members.
Also, to safeguard your finances against possible medical emergencies, invest in a critical illness cover. The cover protects against severe illness and gives financial assistance. A health insurance plan is also recommended to cover the expensive hospitalisation bills incurred in a medical contingency. Choose from the different types of health insurance plans for complete protection. When it comes to critical illness coverage, you can opt for a rider or a standalone plan, depending on your needs.
So, this National Vaccination Day, take a pledge to get vaccinated against diseases that can be prevented. Get your family members vaccinated, too, so that you and they can enjoy a healthy life free from vaccine-preventable diseases. Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. full_pdf_text:
The Significance of Vaccination
Vaccines are an effective way to prevent infectious diseases1. Vaccines prevent up to 3 million deaths worldwide every year1. The World Health Organization (WHO) has listed vaccine hesitancy as one of the biggest threats to global health1. Vaccine hesitancy is where people with access to vaccines delay or refuse vaccination1.
Thus, it is important to urge households to get vaccinated to be protected against diseases like smallpox, polio, Hepatitis, flu, etc. Here are some reasons why you should consider getting vaccinated –
1. To prevent diseases:
One of the primary reasons for vaccination is to avoid possible illnesses that might arise if you are not vaccinated. Vaccination reduces the possibility of acquiring illnesses against which they are built like Hepatitis, typhoid, flu, polio, etc. Even recent illnesses like the Coronavirus infection may be prevented or can be limited if you are vaccinated. These diseases might cause severe physiological losses. Such losses can be avoided through vaccines. 2. For overall safety:
Being vaccinated reduces the chances of contracting the specified illnesses and keeps you safe. For children, vaccinations are required for wellbeing of their overall health and development. 3. For immunity building:
Vaccines contain bacteria and viruses in dormant forms to prompt the immune system to respond against reactions caused by actual pathogen. 4. To reduce the probability of death:
Some illnesses, like COVID, for instance, can develop into serious medical complications that might even cause death. As such, being vaccinated against such deadly diseases can reduce the probability of death. 5. To avoid the possibility of infections:
Some diseases can be contagious. So, if one non-vaccinated individual catches such a disease which is communicable, he/she can infect his/her family members and make them fall sick. In extreme cases, the family member can even lose their life or develop severe physiological conditions. 6. Vaccines are developed after research and trials:
The vaccines are developed after proper research and trials. Moreover, the efficacy of the vaccine has also been endorsed by recognised medical councils and institutions. As such, vaccines are generally considered safe for the masses. 7. To safeguard your loved ones:
Lastly, by vaccinating your family members, you can ensure safety of their health. You may avoid preventable diseases.
To safeguard your finances against possible medical emergencies, consider opting for a critical illness cover/rider while buying a life insurance policy. The cover protects against specified illness and gives financial assistance as stated under the policy. When it comes to critical illness coverage, you may opt for a rider or a standalone plan, depending on your needs.
So, take a pledge to get vaccinated against diseases that can be prevented. Get your family members vaccinated, too, so that you and your family can enjoy a healthy life free from vaccine-preventable diseases. References: 1https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/vaccinations/why-vaccination-is-safe-and-important/ Disclaimer: The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
